detail: 4 of ~16 task units done (T2+T2b, T3+T4, T6+T13partial); 1203 tests green; user flagged that grammar-parser still accepts :Tag(args) pattern syntax — full canonical transition needs scope expansion before T8/T13/T14
reflection: Key finding: T6's FMPL parser rejection is necessary but not sufficient. The grammar parser at grammar/parser.rs:1309-1140 has its own :Tag(child_patterns) syntax that emits Pattern::TagMatch — independent of Expr::Tagged. Migrating grammar action bodies (test fixtures, tavern demo) was sufficient to make 1203 tests green, but the grammar PATTERN side of those fixtures is still legacy. Pattern::TagMatch has 27 references across compiler.rs/vm.rs/grammar runtime/trampoline/pattern/repr — deleting it (T14) is bigger than originally scoped. Second key finding: FMPL inline pattern blocks (cmd @ { pat => body, ... }) can't disambiguate end-of-body from start of next [...]-pattern without explicit separators. Discovered when tavern dispatch arms tried '[:Talk, n] => ...' on a line after 'desc' — parser tried to make 'desc[:Talk, ...]' postfix index. Fix was adding ';' separators in tavern.fmpl. Could improve parser look-ahead later but tabled for now.
